name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Set the barbell just below shoulder height in the squat rack, and load with weight plates.
Step under the bar, resting it across your upper back/traps. Grip the bar firmly.
Unrack the bar and take one step back with each foot. Position feet slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Inhale, engage your core, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the floor or lower, keeping your spine neutral and knees tracking over toes.
Drive through your heels to return to the starting position. Exhale as you press up.
formTips: Keep your chest upright and avoid rounding your lower back.
Never let your knees cave inward; actively push them outward.
Keep your heels flat on the floor throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a dumbbell vertically by one end, cupping your hands under the weight head at chest height.
Keep your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Set feet shoulder-width apart, toes flared slightly out.
Lower into a squat by pushing your hips back, keeping your chest tall and dumbbell close to your sternum.
Go down until your elbows touch the inside of your knees (parallel thigh depth).
Push back up to standing by driving through your mid-foot.
formTips: Keep the weight pressed tight against your chest to prevent leaning forward.
Focus on dropping your hips straight down between your heels.

name: Machine Leg Press
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine and place your feet shoulder-width apart on the center of the sled.
Lower the safety keys and grasp the handles on the sides.
Slowly bend your knees to lower the sled toward your chest until your knees are at about a 90-degree angle.
Avoid letting your lower back or tailbone lift off the padded seat.
Push through your mid-foot/heels to press the sled back to the starting position without locking your knees.
formTips: Never fully lock out or hyperextend your knees at the top.
Ensure your lower back stays firmly pinned against the backrest throughout.

name: Dumbbell Box Squat
instructions: Place a sturdy box or flat bench behind you.
Hold dumbbells at your sides or a single dumbbell at chest height.
Lower yourself slowly into a squat, keeping your hips tracking backwards until your glutes gently touch the box.
Pause for a split second on the box without completely releasing body tension or slumping.
Drive back up to a standing position by contracting your glutes and quads.
formTips: Touch the box lightly; do not sit back fully and lose muscle engagement.
Ensure your knees track in line with your toes, preventing them from wobbling.

name: Bodyweight Air Squat
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, chest upright, and arms in front of you.
Lower your hips down and back, maintaining a flat spine.
Go down until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your feet to return to the starting position.
formTips: Keep your bodyweight balanced in your midfoot and heels.
Keep your core tight to support your back.

name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench, feet firmly planted on the ground.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Unrack the bar and hold it directly over your chest.
Lower the bar slowly under control to your mid-chest level.
Keep your elbows tucked at roughly a 45-degree angle to your torso.
Press the bar back up powerfully until arms are extended, keeping your shoulders retracted into the bench.
formTips: Keep your shoulder blades pulled down and together (retracted).
Do not flare your elbows out to 90 degrees as this puts immense stress on the shoulders.
Ensure your wrists are neutral and stacked under the bar.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench holding a dumbbell on each knee.
Lie back carefully, kicking the weights up to your chest level one at a time.
Position your palms facing forward (or angled slightly inward for joint comfort).
Press the weights up over your chest, keeping your shoulder blades pulled back.
Lower the dumbbells under control until they are level with your chest, feeling a stretch in the pecs.
Press them back up to the starting position.
formTips: Angling your palms slightly inward (45 degrees) is much safer for the shoulder joint.
Control the lowering phase and do not let the dumbbells drift too wide.

name: Neutral Grip Dumbbell Press
instructions: Lie back on a flat bench with dumbbells held over your chest.
Rotate your wrists so your palms are facing each other (neutral grip).
Keep your elbows tucked close to your ribcage as you lower the weights.
Press the dumbbells straight up to arm's length.
This neutral position reduces rotation and strain on both shoulder sockets and wrist joints.
formTips: Do not let the elbows flare outward at all; keep them tracking alongside your ribcage.
Perfect for individuals with mild shoulder or wrist impingements.

name: Machine Chest Press
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles align with your mid-chest.
Sit back firmly, pressing your back and head into the pad.
Grasp the handles, set your feet flat on the floor.
Push the handles away from your body until your arms are extended, but do not lock your elbows.
Slowly return to the start position, feeling a stretch in the chest.
formTips: Ensure your shoulders stay glued to the backrest; do not push your shoulders forward.
Adjust the grip for comfort - neutral handles are often available and shoulder-friendly.

name: Standard Push-Up
instructions: Get into a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and feet together.
Create a straight line from your head to your heels; engage your glutes and core.
Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, keeping your elbows tucked at a 45-degree angle.
Push up through your palms to return to the starting position.
formTips: Do not let your hips sag or your lower back arch excessively.
If you experience wrist pain, perform these on push-up handles or dumbbells to keep wrists neutral.

name: Incline Push-Up
instructions: Place your hands shoulder-width apart on an elevated surface like a bench, box, or stable table.
Step your feet back into an incline plank position, keeping your spine straight.
Lower your chest to the edge of the elevated surface under control.
Push yourself back to the starting position by extending your elbows.
formTips: Ensure the platform is stable and will not slip.
Provides an excellent alternative for beginners who cannot do full floor push-ups, or for individuals rehabbing shoulder injuries.

name: Push-Up on Handles/Dumbbells
instructions: Place two dumbbells or push-up handles on the floor, aligned with your hands in a neutral position (palms facing each other).
Grip the handles tightly and assume a high plank position.
Lower your chest down between the handles, keeping your wrists locked straight (no bending).
Press back up to the top of the plank.
formTips: This is a perfect cure for wrist pain, as it keeps the hand aligned with the forearm.
Maintain strict core stability; do not look down or let hips sag.

name: Barbell Deadlift
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, with your mid-foot positioned directly under the barbell.
Hinge at your hips and bend your knees slightly to grasp the bar with a shoulder-width grip.
Flatten your back completely, pull your chest up, and pull your shoulder blades down and back (engage lats).
Take a deep belly breath and brace your core.
Drive through your legs to lift the bar, keeping it scraping close to your shins, and stand up tall.
Squeeze your glutes at the top without hyperextending your lower back.
Return the bar to the floor by hinging at the hips and then bending knees once the bar clears them.
formTips: Your spine must remain neutral; NEVER round your lower back under heavy load.
Keep the bar tight to your legs; if it drifts forward, it increases back strain.
Do not shrug or pull with your arms; they act simply as anchors.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand tall holding dumbbells in front of your thighs, palms facing your legs.
Keep feet hip-width apart, knees slightly unlocked (soft knees).
Hinge backward at your hips, sliding the dumbbells down the front of your legs.
Keep your back flat and shoulder blades squeezed together.
Go down until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings (usually mid-shin level).
Drive your hips forward, squeezing your glutes to return to a standing position.
formTips: This is a hinge, not a squat. Do not bend your knees further once they are unlocked.
Keep the dumbbells close to your shins; do not let them float forward.

name: Cable Rope Pull-Through
instructions: Set a cable pulley to the lowest setting and attach a rope.
Face away from the machine, reach between your legs to hold the rope handles.
Take a few steps forward to create tension.
Assume a hip-width stance. Hinge forward at the hips, letting the cable pull your hands backwards under your hips.
Keep your back straight and knees slightly bent.
Squeeze your glutes and push your hips forward to stand back up, pulling the rope with you.
formTips: The arms should remain straight; do not pull with your upper body.
Focus entirely on your glutes pushing forward. An excellent safe alternative to deadlifts for back issues.

name: Bodyweight Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back on a yoga mat with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, about hip-width apart.
Keep your arms down at your sides.
Squeeze your glutes, press through your heels, and lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Avoid arching your lower back; form a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, contracting your glutes firmly.
Slowly lower your hips back to the floor.
formTips: Do not push off your shoulders; drive exclusively through your heels.
Keep your abdominal muscles braced to protect your lower back from arching.

name: Bodyweight Pull-Up
instructions: Grasp the pull-up bar with an overhand grip (palms facing away), hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hang with arms fully extended and shoulders packed down.
Inhale, brace your core, and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Pull your body upward by driving your elbows down toward your ribs, until your chin clears the bar.
Lower yourself slowly under control back to a dead hang.
formTips: Do not kick, swing, or kip your legs to gain momentum.
Keep your chest flared up toward the bar throughout the lift.

name: Cable Lat Pulldown
instructions: Sit in the lat pulldown station and adjust the knee pads so you are locked in securely.
Grasp the wide bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lean back slightly (10-15 degrees) and pull your shoulders down and back.
Pull the bar down to your collarbone, focusing on pulling your elbows into your side pockets.
Let the bar rise slowly back to the starting position under control, fully stretching the lats.
formTips: Do not use momentum or rock your body back and forth to pull the weight.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom of the rep.

name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one knee and your same-side hand on a flat bench for support. Keep your spine flat, parallel to the bench.
Grasp a dumbbell with your opposite hand, letting it hang straight down.
Pull the dumbbell up to your hip pocket, keeping your elbow tucked close to your body.
Focus on driving your elbow back rather than pulling with your hand.
Lower the dumbbell slowly to the starting position, stretching the lat fully.
formTips: Do not rotate your torso at the top; keep your shoulders square to the bench.
Avoid letting your shoulder socket drop out of alignment at the bottom.

name: Dumbbell Chest Supported Row
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline.
Lie face down with your chest firmly supported against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Let dumbbells hang straight down at your sides.
Pull your shoulder blades together and row the dumbbells upwards, driving your elbows back.
Lower the weights slowly under control.
formTips: This is the single best horizontal pull for lower back pain, as it removes spinal compression.
Keep your neck neutral; do not look up.

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Sit at the cable row station, placing your feet on the platforms with knees slightly bent.
Reach forward and grasp the close-grip V-handle.
Sit back with an upright torso, shoulders pulled down and back.
Pull the handle toward your abdomen by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Return the handle slowly to the starting position, maintaining a straight, stable spine.
formTips: Do not lean backward or forward excessively to cheat the movement.
Keep your shoulders depressed away from your ears to prevent upper trap dominance.

name: Resistance Band Seated Row
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you.
Loop a resistance band around the soles of your feet.
Hold the handles/ends of the band in each hand with arms extended.
Sit up tall with core engaged. Pull the band back toward your torso, leading with your elbows.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly release the tension back to the starting position.
formTips: Ensure the band is locked securely around your feet so it cannot slip off.
If you have lower back stiffness, sit on a rolled-up towel or bend your knees slightly.

name: Resistance Band Chest Press
instructions: Anchor the resistance band at chest height behind you (e.g., in a doorway or wrapped around a post).
Face away from the anchor and step forward until there is tension on the band.
Hold the handles at chest height, elbows bent and tucked.
Press the band straight forward until your arms are extended.
Return slowly to the starting position.
formTips: Stand in a staggered stance (one foot forward) to maintain solid balance.
Keep your core tight; do not let the band pull you backward.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on an upright utility bench supporting your back.
Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with an overhand grip, elbows bent.
Press the dumbbells straight up overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Keep your back pressed firmly into the bench.
Lower the dumbbells back down slowly to shoulder height.
formTips: Avoid letting your elbows flare directly to the side; keep them angled 30 degrees forward.
Do not arch your lower back off the bench as you press up.

name: Single-Arm Landmine Press
instructions: Anchor one end of a barbell in a landmine attachment or wedge it safely into a corner.
Load the opposite end with weight plates.
Stand facing the bar, holding the loaded sleeve in one hand at shoulder height.
Step your foot back on the same side (staggered stance) for stability.
Press the barbell up and forward at a 45-degree angle until your arm is extended.
Lower it back down slowly under control.
formTips: This is an excellent, safe overhead press alternative for people with shoulder impingements or poor overhead mobility.
Lean forward slightly into the bar as you press.

name: Bodyweight Bird Dog
instructions: Begin on all fours on a yoga mat: hands under shoulders, knees under hips.
Maintain a flat, neutral spine.
Slowly extend your right arm straight forward and your left leg straight backward.
Keep your hips square to the ground; do not let your back sag.
Squeeze your glutes and hold the extension for 2 seconds.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat with the opposite arm and leg.
formTips: Focus on length, not height. Reach your fingers forward and heel backward.
Do not arch your lower back as you lift your leg. The ultimate back rehabilitation exercise.

name: Bodyweight Dead Bug
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a yoga mat.
Raise your arms straight up toward the ceiling and lift your knees to a 90-degree angle (shin parallel to floor).
Flatten your lower back completely against the floor - there should be no space.
Slowly lower your right arm backward and your left leg forward until they are just above the floor.
Maintain your lower back's contact with the floor. Do not let it arch.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
formTips: The entire key is keeping your lower back pressed flat into the ground.
Move slowly and with full control; do not rush the movement.

name: Forearm Plank
instructions: Place your forearms on a yoga mat, elbows directly beneath your shoulders.
Step your feet back, lifting your knees so your weight is supported on forearms and toes.
Align your neck, shoulders, hips, and heels into a straight line.
Contract your core, squeeze your glutes, and tuck your pelvis slightly.
Hold this position, breathing steadily.
formTips: Do not look up or let your hips sag toward the ground.
Keep your shoulders pushed active away from the floor (no collapsing in the shoulder blades).

name: Cable Face Pull
instructions: Set a cable pulley to upper chest/face height and attach a double-rope handle.
Grasp the rope ends with a neutral grip (thumbs facing back).
Step back to create tension and stand with feet hip-width apart.
Pull the center of the rope directly toward your nose.
As you pull, flare your elbows out and pull your hands apart, rotating your wrists externally (thumbs end up pointing behind you).
Squeeze your rear shoulders and upper back, then slowly release.
formTips: Keep your chest proud and do not stick your chin forward to meet the rope.
Excellent posture corrector and shoulder joint stabilizer.

name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand tall holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing forward.
Keep your elbows locked tight into your ribs.
Curl the weights upward toward your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Squeeze your biceps hard at the top.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to a full extension.
formTips: Do not swing your torso or raise your elbows forward to cheat the weight.
Keep your wrists locked straight throughout the curl.

name: Cable Rope Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Attach a rope attachment to a high pulley of a cable station.
Grip the rope handles, step back slightly, and lean your chest slightly forward.
Lock your elbows into your sides; they must stay here.
Push the rope down towards your thighs, splitting the ends of the rope apart at the bottom.
Squeeze your triceps, then slowly return to the top, bending only at the elbows.
formTips: Never let your elbows flare out or move forward.
Control the weight on the way up; do not let it drop.

name: Dumbbell Step-Up
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells in your hands (or bodyweight only) in front of a flat bench or box.
Place your right foot fully onto the bench.
Press through your right heel to lift your body onto the bench, standing up straight.
Avoid pushing off with your trailing foot; let the lead leg do 100% of the work.
Step down carefully with your left foot first, then the right.
formTips: Keep your knee tracking in line with your toe on the working leg.
Perform all reps on one leg before switching, or alternate as programmed.

name: World's Greatest Stretch
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders.
Step your right foot forward to the outside of your right hand, keeping your back leg straight and heel lifted.
Lift your right hand off the floor and rotate your torso to the right, reaching your right hand straight up to the ceiling.
Follow your hand with your gaze to stretch your chest, shoulder, and upper back.
Bring your right hand down, placing your elbow inside your right foot to deepen the hip stretch.
Return your right hand to the floor and step back to the plank position. Repeat on the left side.
formTips: Keep your back leg as straight as possible by squeezing your quad.
Move slowly and synchronize your movements with smooth, deep breaths.
Don't force the shoulder rotation; let your upper spine rotate naturally.

name: Cobra Stretch Hold
instructions: Lie face down on a yoga mat with your legs extended behind you and the tops of your feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands on the mat directly under your shoulders, elbows tucked close to your body.
Press down through your hands and gently straighten your arms to lift your chest off the floor.
Keep your pelvis and thighs pressed firmly into the mat.
Relax your shoulders down away from your ears and extend your neck gently, gazing forward or slightly up.
Hold this position, breathing deeply into your abdomen.
formTips: Do not shrug your shoulders; keep them pulled down and back.
If your lower back pinches, lower your torso slightly or rest on your forearms instead (Sphinx pose).

name: Cat-Cow Mobility Flow
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees on a yoga mat, with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
Inhale deeply, dropping your belly towards the mat, lifting your chin and chest, and looking up (Cow pose).
As you exhale, draw your belly button into your spine, round your back toward the ceiling, and tuck your chin to your chest (Cat pose).
Continue flowing smoothly between Cat and Cow, synchronizing each movement with a full breath cycle.
formTips: Distribute weight evenly through your hands; don't dump all your weight into your wrists.
Keep the movement fluid and avoid jerky motions.

name: Child's Pose Rest Hold
instructions: Kneel on the floor, bring your big toes to touch, and sit back on your heels.
Separate your knees about hip-width or slightly wider.
Exhale and lower your torso forward between your thighs, walking your hands out in front of you.
Rest your forehead gently on the mat and reach your arms forward, stretching your lats.
Deepen the stretch by sinking your hips closer to your heels with every exhale.
formTips: If sitting on your heels is uncomfortable, place a pillow or folded blanket under your hips.
Breathe slowly and let your back expand laterally with each inhale.

name: Pigeon Stretch (Glute Opener)
instructions: From a push-up or all-fours position, bring your right knee forward and place it near your right wrist.
Position your right shin parallel to the front of your mat (or angled slightly toward your left hip if tight).
Slide your left leg back, straightening it completely, with your thigh and foot flat on the floor.
Keep your hips square. Walk your hands forward and lower your upper body to your forearms or all the way to the mat.
Hold, taking deep, slow breaths to sink into the glute stretch.
formTips: Do not roll onto your right hip; keep your hips centered and level.
Flex your front foot slightly to protect your knee.

name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
instructions: Kneel on your left knee on a yoga mat, with your right foot flat on the floor in front of you, creating 90-degree angles with both legs.
Keep your hands on your hips and make sure your posture is tall and upright.
Tuck your pelvis under (posterior pelvic tilt) and squeeze your left glute.
Gently shift your weight slightly forward until you feel a deep stretch in the front of your left hip/thigh.
Hold the position and breathe deeply.
formTips: Do not arch your lower back to shift forward; the stretch comes from tucking the pelvis and squeezing the rear glute.
Place a cushion under your knee if you experience discomfort from the floor.

name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart.
Extend your right leg slightly forward, resting your heel on the floor with your toes pointing up.
Bend your left knee slightly and hinge forward at your hips, keeping your spine straight and hands on your left thigh.
Gently lower your chest toward your right leg until you feel a comfortable stretch along your hamstring.
Hold, then switch sides.
formTips: Keep your lower back flat; do not round your spine to reach lower.
Keep your hips square throughout the stretch.

name: Doorway Chest & Shoulder Stretch
instructions: Stand in an open doorway.
Raise your arms up so your elbows are at a 90-degree angle and your forearms rest on the door frame.
Slowly step forward with one foot until you feel a deep, comfortable stretch across your chest and shoulders.
Keep your head up, shoulders down, and your core engaged.
Hold the stretch and focus on deep breathing.
formTips: Do not lean too far forward; only go until you feel a moderate stretch, never pain.
Keep your shoulder blades pinched slightly together.

name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step forward with your right foot, landing on the heel first, and lower your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees.
Your back knee should hover just above the floor, and your front knee should track behind your toes.
Push through your right heel and step forward with your left foot to complete a walking stride and lunge on the left leg.
Continue walking forward, alternating legs.
formTips: Keep your torso upright and avoid leaning forward excessively.
Engage your core to maintain balance and prevent side-to-side leaning.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Shoulder Raise
instructions: Stand tall holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing inward.
With a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides in a wide arc.
Lift the weights until your arms are parallel to the floor (shoulder level).
Squeeze your shoulders at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
formTips: Do not use momentum or swing your hips to lift the weights.
Lead with your elbows and keep your shoulders pressed down, avoiding shrugging your neck.

name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand upright holding dumbbells at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Keep your elbows locked close to your ribs.
Curl the weights upward while maintaining the neutral grip until your forearms are almost vertical.
Squeeze your brachialis hard, then slowly lower the weights to full extension.
formTips: Do not swing your arms or bend your wrists.
Keep your posture tall and squeeze your core.

name: Bodyweight Russian Twist
instructions: Sit on your yoga mat with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Lean back slightly at about a 45-degree angle, keeping your spine straight and core tight.
Clasp your hands together in front of your chest. For an extra challenge, lift your feet off the floor.
Rotate your torso slowly to the right, touching your hands to the floor beside your hip.
Engage your obliques, rotate back through the center, and touch the floor on your left side.
formTips: Rotate from your torso and ribcage, not just your arms.
Keep your spine straight; do not slouch or hunch your shoulders.

name: Arnold Dumbbell Press
instructions: Sit on an incline or flat bench with back support, holding dumbbells in front of you at chest height with palms facing your chest.
As you press the dumbbells overhead, rotate your wrists so that your palms face forward at the peak of the movement.
Lower the weights back down to the starting position, rotating your wrists back so your palms face you again.
formTips: Keep your back firmly against the bench pad.
Control the weights on the descent; do not drop them quickly.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Kneel on a yoga mat and place the ab wheel on the floor in front of you.
Grasp the handles firmly, engage your core, and slowly roll the wheel forward, extending your body into a straight line.
Go as far as you can while maintaining a flat back, then roll the wheel back by contracting your abs.
formTips: Do not let your hips sag or your lower back arch.
Start with a shorter range of motion (rolling towards a wall) until you build strength.

name: Weighted Bench Dip
instructions: Place a sturdy flat bench behind you, resting your hands on the edge with fingers pointing forward.
Extend your legs in front of you with your heels on the ground or on another bench.
Slowly lower your hips by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor, then press back up.
formTips: Keep your back close to the bench throughout the movement.
Do not lower yourself too deep to avoid extreme shoulder strain.

name: Machine Leg Extension
instructions: Sit in the leg extension machine and adjust the pad so it rests on top of your lower shins/ankles.
Grasp the handles on the sides of the seat to anchor your body.
Extend your legs fully by contracting your quadriceps, holding the peak contraction for a split second.
Slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
formTips: Ensure your back is flat against the backrest.
Do not use explosive momentum to swing the weight up.

name: Lying Machine Leg Curl
instructions: Lie face down on the leg curl machine, positioning the roller pad just below your calf muscles.
Grasp the handles and keep your torso flat against the bench.
Curl your legs upward toward your glutes by flexing your hamstrings.
Slowly return to the starting position under control.
formTips: Keep your hips pressed firmly against the pad; do not let your lower back arch to help lift the weight.
Perform the movement slowly, focusing on hamstring tension.

name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or weight plate with your heels hanging off.
Slowly lower your heels below the level of the step to feel a deep calf stretch.
Press up on your toes as high as possible, contracting your calves at the peak.
Lower back down slowly to the starting position.
formTips: Hold a wall or rail for balance if needed.
Pause at the bottom stretch and the top contraction to maximize muscle engagement.

name: Dumbbell Chest Fly
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding dumbbells above your chest with palms facing each other, arms slightly bent.
Slowly lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Squeeze your chest muscles to pull the dumbbells back up to the starting position along the same arc.
formTips: Maintain a constant slight bend in your elbows; do not lock them out.
Only go down as far as is comfortable to avoid shoulder injury.

name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench, lean forward slightly, and rest the back of your upper arm against the inside of your thigh.
Hold a dumbbell in your hand with your arm fully extended downward.
Curl the weight up toward your face by flexing your bicep, keeping your elbow locked against your thigh.
Slowly lower the weight back down to full extension.
formTips: Ensure your upper body remains still; do not swing or rock your torso.
Keep the movement strictly in the elbow joint.
